content.problemStatement:
Mary loves binary strings.

Given a binary string SSS, she defines the beauty of the string as the bitwise XOR of **decimal representations** of **all substrings** of SSS.

Find the beauty of string SSS. Since the answer can be huge, print it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.

For example, the decimal representation of binary string 110111011101 is 1⋅23+1⋅22+0⋅21+1⋅20=8+4+0+1=131\cdot 2^3 + 1\cdot 2^2 + 0\cdot 2^1 + 1\cdot 2^0 = 8 + 4 + 0+1 = 131⋅23+1⋅22+0⋅21+1⋅20=8+4+0+1=13. Kindly refer sample explanation for more such examples.

A string AAA is a substring of a string BBB if AAA can be obtained from BBB by deleting several (possibly zero) characters from the beginning and several (possibly zero) characters from the end.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of test cases follow.
First line of each test case contains one integer NNN - the length of the binary string.
Second line of each test case contains the binary string SSS.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line, beauty of the string modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111: All substrings of the string S=10S = 10S=10 are [1,0,10][1, 0, 10][1,0,10]. The decimal representation of these substrings is denoted by the array [1,0,2][1, 0, 2][1,0,2]. Bitwise XOR of all these values is 1⊕0⊕2=31 \oplus 0 \oplus 2 = 31⊕0⊕2=3.
Test Case 222: All substrings of the string S=101S = 101S=101 are [1,0,1,10,01,101][1, 0, 1, 10, 01, 101][1,0,1,10,01,101]. The decimal representation of these substrings is denoted by the array [1,0,1,2,1,5][1, 0, 1, 2, 1, 5][1,0,1,2,1,5]. Bitwise XOR of all these values is: 1⊕0⊕1⊕2⊕1⊕5=61 \oplus 0 \oplus 1 \oplus 2 \oplus 1 \oplus 5 = 61⊕0⊕1⊕2⊕1⊕5=6.
Test Case 333: All substrings of the string S=1111S = 1111S=1111 are [1,1,1,1,11,11,11,111,111,1111][1, 1, 1, 1, 11, 11, 11, 111, 111, 1111][1,1,1,1,11,11,11,111,111,1111]. The decimal representation of these substrings is denoted by the array [1,1,1,1,3,3,3,7,7,15][1, 1, 1, 1, 3, 3, 3, 7, 7, 15][1,1,1,1,3,3,3,7,7,15]. Bitwise XOR of all these values is: 1⊕1⊕1⊕1⊕3⊕3⊕3⊕7⊕7⊕15=121 \oplus 1 \oplus 1 \oplus 1 \oplus 3 \oplus 3 \oplus 3 \oplus 7 \oplus 7 \oplus 15 = 121⊕1⊕1⊕1⊕3⊕3⊕3⊕7⊕7⊕15=12.
